documentation.value: Creates the HTML element specified by tagName.
A string specifying the type of element to create (e.g., 'div', 'span', 'p').
Returns the element that has the specified ID attribute, or null if no such element exists.
The ID of the element to locate.
Returns the first element within the document that matches the specified CSS selector(s).
A CSS selector string (e.g., '.class', '#id', 'div > p').
Returns a static NodeList of all elements matching the specified CSS selector(s).
A CSS selector string.
Sets the value of an attribute on the specified element. If the attribute already exists, the value is updated.
The name of the attribute (e.g., 'class', 'id', 'src').
The value to assign to the attribute.
Returns the value of a specified attribute on the element, or null if the attribute does not exist.
The name of the attribute to retrieve.

signatures.label: element.addEventListener(type: string, listener: EventListener, options?: AddEventListenerOptions): void

documentation.value: Registers an event handler on the element for the specified event type.
The event type to listen for (e.g., 'click', 'input', 'submit').
The callback function invoked when the event fires.
Optional object with capture, once, passive, and signal properties.

signatures.label: element.removeEventListener(type: string, listener: EventListener, options?: EventListenerOptions): void

documentation.value: Removes a previously registered event listener from the element.
The event type of the listener to remove.
The callback function to remove.
Optional object specifying characteristics of the listener.
Adds one or more CSS class names to the element's class list.
One or more class names to add.
Removes one or more CSS class names from the element's class list.
One or more class names to remove.
Toggles the specified class name on the element. If force is provided, adds the class when true and removes when false.
The class name to toggle.
If true, add the class; if false, remove it.
Parses the text as HTML and inserts the resulting nodes at the specified position relative to the element.
One of 'beforebegin', 'afterbegin', 'beforeend', 'afterend'.
The HTML string to parse and insert.
